headline: A PKCS#8 private-key parser joins the pki.schema family.
summary: pki.schema.pkcs8 parses PKCS#8 private keys — plaintext PrivateKeyInfo (RFC 5208 §5) / OneAsymmetricKey (RFC 5958 §2) and, recognized-but-not-decrypted, EncryptedPrivateKeyInfo (RFC 5958 §3). It turns DER or PEM into a structured object: version, the private-key algorithm, the raw private-key bytes, the optional attributes, and — for a v2 key — the optional public key. pki.schema.parse now detects and routes both plaintext and encrypted keys. The private-key OCTET STRING is kept raw, so the caller decodes the algorithm-specific inner key using the surfaced algorithm OID and an unknown or future key type never fails the parse.

Added: pki.schema.pkcs8.parse — a PKCS#8 PrivateKeyInfo / OneAsymmetricKey parser per RFC 5208 §5 and RFC 5958 §2. It turns a DER Buffer or a 'PRIVATE KEY' PEM string into { version, privateKeyAlgorithm, privateKey, attributes, publicKey }, where privateKey is the raw OCTET STRING content (the inner RSA/EC/curve key, decoded by the caller via privateKeyAlgorithm.oid) and publicKey is present only for a v2 key. The version must be v1 (0) or v2 (1), and a [1] public key is permitted only in a v2 key (both directions enforced). A malformed key throws a typed Pkcs8Error (pkcs8/*); a leaf-level codec fault surfaces as asn1/*. pki.schema.pkcs8.pemDecode / pemEncode handle the PEM envelope.

pki.schema.pkcs8.parseEncrypted — recognizes an EncryptedPrivateKeyInfo ('ENCRYPTED PRIVATE KEY') and surfaces its encryptionAlgorithm and raw encryptedData. Decryption (PBES2/PBKDF2 + a passphrase) is a separate concern and is not performed here. pki.schema.parse routes an encrypted key to this member and never mis-parses it as a plaintext key.

pki.asn1.read.enumerated's sibling pki.asn1.read.bitStringImplicit and the pki.schema.engine.implicitBitString(tag) leaf — read a context-tagged IMPLICIT BIT STRING (the shape a PKCS#8 OneAsymmetricKey public key [1] takes).
